strengths: Nearly two decades of hands-on full-stack experience, with recent senior TypeScript/Node.js product work at re:cinq, Optilyz (TypeScript, React, MongoDB, AWS CDK) and RIDE Capital (Node, TypeScript, GraphQL) — well beyond the 6+ years required

Directly relevant AI-agent development experience: currently building MCP (Model Context Protocol) code generators and frameworks supporting agentic workflows, a rare match for the JD's explicit AI-agent requirement

Deep testing and quality culture — practiced TDD, pairing, CI/CD and trunk-based development, authored test libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial) and taught multi-day testing/Selenium courses, aligning with the role's emphasis on code review, clean code and testing expertise
concerns: No explicit Nest.js experience listed; React appears only once (Optilyz), with much of the frontend history in Ember.js
Kotlin and Python are absent from the skills list, and Vitest/Jest/Playwright are not explicitly named despite strong general testing credentials
Significant career time invested in the niche DLang ecosystem, so depth in the exact primary stack (Nest.js, Vitest) would need validation in interview
response.justification: This candidate is a compelling fit for a Senior Fullstack role building internal automation tooling: they far exceed the experience bar, have current production TypeScript/Node.js and React work, cloud (AWS, Kubernetes, Docker) and database (MongoDB, MySQL, SQL) depth, and — most distinctively — active, hands-on AI-agent infrastructure work with MCP code generators that directly satisfies the JD's hardest-to-find requirement. Their polyglot background (TypeScript, Java, C/C++, D, Swift) matches the multi-language expectation, and a long record of workshops, courses and open-source maintainership (GISCollective, used by Green Map) evidences the communication, collaboration and customer-oriented delivery the role calls for. Gaps around Nest.js and Vitest specifically are narrow tooling deltas that a developer of this breadth and testing pedigree can be expected to close quickly.
